-              <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-3">
-                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.needWaiter} icon={<Bell size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-mictlan-red" onClick={() => handleToggleRequest(table, 'needWaiter')} />

-              onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); resetTable(table.fireId); addLog(`Table T-${table.name || table.id} réinitialisée`, 'info', userData, 'tables'); }}

+import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import { useTables } from '../hooks/useTables';
+import { updateTableStatus, toggleTableRequest, resetTable, startOccupation } from '../firebase/tableActions';
+import { useSettings } from '../hooks/useSettings'; // Import useSettings
+
+import { 
+  Bell, Utensils, Moon, RefreshCw, FileText, CreditCard, 
+  RotateCcw, Clock, Users, Timer
+} from 'lucide-react';
+import Card from './Card';
+
+const TimeSinceInteraction = ({ timestamp }) => {
+  const [timeStr, setTimeStr] = useState("0m 0s");
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!timestamp) return;
+    const calc = () => {
+      const date = timestamp.toDate ? timestamp.toDate() : new Date(timestamp);
+      const diffMs = Math.max(0, new Date() - date);
+      const totalSeconds = Math.floor(diffMs / 1000);
+      const minutes = Math.floor(totalSeconds / 60);
+      const seconds = totalSeconds % 60;
+      setTimeStr(`${minutes}m ${seconds}s`);
+    };
+    calc();
+    const inv = setInterval(calc, 1000);
+    return () => clearInterval(inv);
+  }, [timestamp]);
+  return <span className="text-[10px] font-black text-mictlan-gold flex items-center gap-1 uppercase"><Clock size={10}/> {timeStr}</span>;
+};
+
+const TotalTime = ({ timestamp }) => {
+  const [timeStr, setTimeStr] = useState("0m 0s");
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!timestamp) return;
+    const calc = () => {
+      const date = timestamp.toDate ? timestamp.toDate() : new Date(timestamp);
+      const diffMs = Math.max(0, new Date() - date);
+      const totalSeconds = Math.floor(diffMs / 1000);
+      const minutes = Math.floor(totalSeconds / 60);
+      const seconds = totalSeconds % 60;
+      setTimeStr(`${minutes}m ${seconds}s`);
+    };
+    calc();
+    const inv = setInterval(calc, 1000);
+    return () => clearInterval(inv);
+  }, [timestamp]);
+  return <span className="text-[10px] font-black text-gray-500 flex items-center gap-1 uppercase"><Timer size={10}/> {timeStr}</span>;
+}
+
+const ToggleButton = ({ active, icon, onClick, color, bgActive }) => (
+  <button 
+    onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onClick(); }}
+    className={`p-3 rounded-xl flex justify-center items-center transition-all border ${
+      active 
+      ? `${bgActive} ${color} border-mictlan-gold shadow-[0_0_10px_rgba(212,175,55,0.3)]` 
+      : 'bg-black/40 text-gray-600 border-gray-800 hover:border-gray-600'
+    }`}
+  >
+    {icon}
+  </button>
+);
+
+export default function TableGrid({ playNotificationSound, showAdminView, userData }) {
+  const { tables, loading } = useTables();
+  const { settings, updateSettings } = useSettings(); // Get and update settings
+  const [viewMode, setViewMode] = useState('linear');
+
+  // Inactivity check effect
+  useEffect(() => {
+    // Only proceed if tables are loaded and a timeout is set
+    if (loading || !tables.length || settings.inactivityTimeout === undefined) return;
+
+    const interval = setInterval(() => {
+      const now = new Date();
+      tables.forEach(table => {
+        if (table.status === 'occupied' && table.lastUpdated) {
+          const lastUpdatedDate = table.lastUpdated.toDate ? table.lastUpdated.toDate() : new Date(table.lastUpdated);
+          const diffMins = Math.floor((now - lastUpdatedDate) / 60000);
+          
+          if (diffMins >= settings.inactivityTimeout) {
+            updateTableStatus(table.fireId, 'tout va bien?');
+          }
+        }
+      });
+    }, 60000); // Check every minute
+
+    return () => {
+      clearInterval(interval);
+    }
+  }, [tables, settings.inactivityTimeout, loading]); // Re-run if tables or timeout setting changes
+
+  // Recurring sound alert effect
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (loading || !settings.enableRecurringAlerts) return;
+
+    const interval = setInterval(() => {
+      const alertedTables = tables.filter(t => ['alert', 'tout va bien?', 'cleaning'].includes(t.status));
+      if (alertedTables.length > 0) {
+        const message = `Alerte ! ${alertedTables.length} table(s) nécessitent une attention.`;
+        playNotificationSound(message);
+      }
+    }, 60000); // Check every minute
+
+    return () => clearInterval(interval);
+  }, [tables, settings.enableRecurringAlerts, loading, playNotificationSound]);
+
+  if (loading) return <div className="p-10 text-center text-mictlan-gold animate-pulse uppercase tracking-widest font-bold">Chargement de la salle...</div>;
+
+  const getStatusStyle = (table) => {
+    if (table.requests?.needWaiter || table.status === 'alert') return 'bg-mictlan-red/10 border-mictlan-red shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(220,38,38,0.2)] animate-pulse';
+    if (table.status === 'tout va bien?') return 'bg-orange-900/20 border-orange-500 shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(249,115,22,0.2)] animate-pulse';
+    if (table.status === 'cleaning') return 'bg-sky-900/20 border-sky-500';
+    if (table.requests?.dontDisturb) return 'bg-purple-900/20 border-purple-500';
+    if (table.requests?.readyToOrder) return 'bg-green-900/20 border-green-500';
+    if (table.requests?.needBill || table.requests?.needTPE) return 'bg-blue-900/20 border-blue-500';
+    if (table.status === 'occupied') return 'bg-mictlan-gold/5 border-mictlan-gold/30';
+    return 'bg-mictlan-dark-gray/30 border-gray-800';
+  };
+
+  const handleLinearClick = (table) => {
+    if (table.status === 'tout va bien?') {
+      updateTableStatus(table.fireId, 'occupied');
+      return;
+    }
+    if (table.status === 'idle') {
+      startOccupation(table.fireId);
+      return;
+    }
+
+    const statusCycle = ['occupied', 'alert', 'cleaning'];
+    const currentIdx = statusCycle.indexOf(table.status);
+    
+    if (currentIdx === -1) {
+      // If the status is not in our main cycle, maybe it's a request status.
+      // For now, we do nothing.
+      return; 
+    }
+    
+    const nextStatus = statusCycle[(currentIdx + 1) % statusCycle.length];
+    updateTableStatus(table.fireId, nextStatus);
+  };
+  
+  const getStatusText = (status) => {
+    switch (status) {
+      case 'idle': return 'Libre';
+      case 'tout va bien?': return 'Tout va bien?';
+      case 'cleaning': return 'À nettoyer';
+      default: return status;
+    }
+  }
+
+
+  return (
+    <Card title="État des Tables" titleIcon={<Users />}>
+      {userData?.role === 'admin' && showAdminView && (
+        <div className="mb-4 p-4 bg-mictlan-dark-gray/20 rounded-lg border border-gray-700 space-y-4">
+          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
+            <label htmlFor="inactivityTimeout" className="text-sm font-black text-mictlan-gold uppercase tracking-[0.2em]">
+              Délai d&apos;inactivité (min) :
+            </label>
+            <input
+              id="inactivityTimeout"
+              type="number"
+              min="1"
+              value={settings.inactivityTimeout || 10}
+              onChange={(e) => updateSettings({ inactivityTimeout: Number(e.target.value) })}
+              className="w-20 p-2 bg-black/40 text-mictlan-gold border border-gray-600 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-mictlan-gold"
+            />
+          </div>
+          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
+            <label htmlFor="soundRateLimit" className="text-sm font-black text-mictlan-gold uppercase tracking-[0.2em]">
+              Délai minimum entre les sons (s) :
+            </label>
+            <input
+              id="soundRateLimit"
+              type="number"
+              min="0"
+              value={settings.soundRateLimit || 5}
+              onChange={(e) => updateSettings({ soundRateLimit: Number(e.target.value) })}
+              className="w-20 p-2 bg-black/40 text-mictlan-gold border border-gray-600 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-mictlan-gold"
+            />
+          </div>
+          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
+            <label htmlFor="recurringAlertInterval" className="text-sm font-black text-mictlan-gold uppercase tracking-[0.2em]">
+              Alerte de rappel toutes les :
+            </label>
+            <input
+              id="recurringAlertInterval"
+              type="number"
+              min="0"
+              value={settings.recurringAlertInterval || 0}
+              onChange={(e) => updateSettings({ recurringAlertInterval: Number(e.target.value) })}
+              className="w-20 p-2 bg-black/40 text-mictlan-gold border border-gray-600 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-mictlan-gold"
+            />
+          </div>
+          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
+            <label htmlFor="enableRecurringAlerts" className="text-sm font-black text-mictlan-gold uppercase tracking-[0.2em]">
+              Activer alerte sonore continue :
+            </label>
+            <input
+              id="enableRecurringAlerts"
+              type="checkbox"
+              checked={settings.enableRecurringAlerts || false}
+              onChange={(e) => updateSettings({ enableRecurringAlerts: e.target.checked })}
+              className="w-6 h-6 bg-black/40 text-mictlan-gold border border-gray-600 rounded-md focus:ring-1 focus:ring-mictlan-gold"
+            />
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      )}
+      <div className="flex justify-end mb-4">
+        <button 
+          onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setViewMode(prev => prev === 'linear' ? 'toggles' : 'linear'); }}
+          className="px-4 py-2 bg-gray-800 text-mictlan-gold rounded-full border border-gray-700 text-[10px] font-black uppercase tracking-widest"
+        >
+          {viewMode === 'toggles' ? 'Vue Cycle' : 'Vue Détaillée'}
+        </button>
+      </div>
+      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3 gap-8 overflow-y-auto max-h-[600px]">
+        {tables.map(table => (
+          <div key={table.fireId} className={`relative p-5 rounded-3xl border-2 transition-all duration-300 ${getStatusStyle(table)}`}>
+            <div className="flex justify-between items-start mb-4">
+              <span className="text-2xl font-black text-mictlan-gold tracking-tighter">T-{table.name || table.id}</span>
+              <div className="flex flex-col items-end gap-1">
+                {table.status !== 'idle' && table.lastUpdated && <TimeSinceInteraction timestamp={table.lastUpdated} />}
+                {table.status !== 'idle' && table.occupiedTimestamp && <TotalTime timestamp={table.occupiedTimestamp} />}
+              </div>
+            </div>
+            {viewMode === 'linear' ? (
+              <div className="h-32 flex flex-col items-center justify-center cursor-pointer group" onClick={() => handleLinearClick(table)}>
+                <span className="text-sm font-black uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-gray-300 group-hover:text-mictlan-gold transition-colors">
+                  {getStatusText(table.status)}
+                </span>
+                <div className="flex gap-2 mt-3">
+                    {table.requests?.needWaiter && <Bell size={14} className="text-mictlan-red animate-bounce" />}
+                    {table.requests?.needBill && <FileText size={14} className="text-blue-400" />}
+                </div>
+              </div>
+            ) : (
+              <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-3">
+                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.needWaiter} icon={<Bell size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-mictlan-red" onClick={() => toggleTableRequest(table.fireId, 'needWaiter', table.requests?.needWaiter)} />
+                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.readyToOrder} icon={<Utensils size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-green-600" onClick={() => toggleTableRequest(table.fireId, 'readyToOrder', table.requests?.readyToOrder)} />
+                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.dontDisturb} icon={<Moon size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-purple-600" onClick={() => toggleTableRequest(table.fireId, 'dontDisturb', table.requests?.dontDisturb)} />
+                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.nextPlate} icon={<RefreshCw size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-orange-500" onClick={() => toggleTableRequest(table.fireId, 'nextPlate', table.requests?.nextPlate)} />
+                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.needBill} icon={<FileText size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-blue-600" onClick={() => toggleTableRequest(table.fireId, 'needBill', table.requests?.needBill)} />
+                <ToggleButton active={table.requests?.needTPE} icon={<CreditCard size={20}/>} color="text-white" bgActive="bg-teal-600" onClick={() => toggleTableRequest(table.fireId, 'needTPE', table.requests?.needTPE)} />
+              </div>
+            )}
+            <button 
+              onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); resetTable(table.fireId); }}
+              className="absolute -top-2 -right-2 p-2 bg-gray-900 border border-gray-700 text-gray-500 hover:text-mictlan-red rounded-full shadow-xl transition-colors"
+            >
+              <RotateCcw size={12} />
+            </button>
+          </div>
+        ))}
+      </div>
+    </Card>
+  );
 }

+import React, { useState } from 'react';
+import { PackagePlus, Trash2 } from 'lucide-react';
+
+export default function TestDataManager({ type }) {
+  const [isPopulating, setIsPopulating] = useState(false);
+  const [isClearing, setIsClearing] = useState(false);
+  const [feedback, setFeedback] = useState('');
+
+  const cloudFunctionsBaseUrl = `https://us-central1-${import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID}.cloudfunctions.net`;
+
+  const handlePopulate = async () => {
+    setIsPopulating(true);
+    setFeedback(`Remplissage des ${type} de test...`);
+
+    const functionName = type === 'products' ? 'populateProducts' : 'populateDishes';
+    const successMessage = type === 'products' ? 'Produits de test ajoutés avec succès !' : 'Plats de test ajoutés avec succès !';
+    const errorMessage = type === 'products' ? 'Erreur lors du remplissage des produits:' : 'Erreur lors du remplissage des plats:';
+
+    try {
+      const response = await fetch(`${cloudFunctionsBaseUrl}/${functionName}`, {
+        method: 'POST',
+        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+        body: JSON.stringify({})
+      });
+      if (!response.ok) {
+        throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${response.status}`);
+      }
+      const result = await response.json();
+      setFeedback(result.message || successMessage);
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error(`${errorMessage}`, error);
+      setFeedback(`${errorMessage} ${error.message}`);
+    } finally {
+      setIsPopulating(false);
+      setTimeout(() => setFeedback(''), 3000);
+    }
+  };
+
+  const handleClear = async () => {
+    if (!window.confirm(`Êtes-vous sûr de vouloir vider la collection de ${type} ? Cette action est irréversible.`)) {
+      return;
+    }
+    setIsClearing(true);
+    setFeedback(`Vidage de la collection de ${type}...`);
+
+    const functionName = type === 'products' ? 'clearProducts' : 'clearDishes'; // Assuming clearDishes exists or will be created
+    const successMessage = type === 'products' ? 'Collection de produits vidée avec succès.' : 'Collection de plats vidée avec succès.';
+    const errorMessage = type === 'products' ? 'Erreur lors du vidage des produits:' : 'Erreur lors du vidage des plats:';
+
+    try {
+      const response = await fetch(`${cloudFunctionsBaseUrl}/${functionName}`, {
+        method: 'POST',
+        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+        body: JSON.stringify({})
+      });
+      if (!response.ok) {
+        throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${response.status}`);
+      }
+      const result = await response.json();
+      setFeedback(result.message || successMessage);
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error(`${errorMessage}`, error);
+      setFeedback(`${errorMessage} ${error.message}`);
+    } finally {
+      setIsClearing(false);
+      setTimeout(() => setFeedback(''), 3000);
+    }
+  };
+
+  const labelText = type === 'products' ? 'Gestion des Produits de Test' : 'Gestion des Plats de Test';
+  const populateButtonText = type === 'products' ? 'Remplir Produits' : 'Remplir Plats';
+  const clearButtonText = type === 'products' ? 'Vider Produits' : 'Vider Plats';
+
+  return (
+    <div className="flex flex-col gap-4">
+      <label className="block text-sm font-black text-mictlan-gold uppercase tracking-[0.2em] mb-2">
+        {labelText}
+      </label>
+      <div className="flex gap-4">
+        <button
+          onClick={handlePopulate}
+          disabled={isPopulating}
+          className="flex-1 bg-black/40 text-gray-500 rounded-lg border border-gray-800 hover:text-mictlan-gold transition-all px-4 py-2 font-bold text-[10px] uppercase flex items-center justify-center gap-2"
+        >
+          <PackagePlus className={`w-3 h-3 ${isPopulating ? 'animate-spin' : ''}`} />
+          {isPopulating ? 'Remplissage...' : populateButtonText}
+        </button>
+        <button
+          onClick={handleClear}
+          disabled={isClearing}
+          className="flex-1 bg-black/40 text-gray-500 rounded-lg border border-gray-800 hover:text-mictlan-red transition-all px-4 py-2 font-bold text-[10px] uppercase flex items-center justify-center gap-2"
+        >
+          <Trash2 className={`w-3 h-3 ${isClearing ? 'animate-spin' : ''}`} />
+          {isClearing ? 'Vidage...' : clearButtonText}
+        </button>
+      </div>
+      {feedback && <p className="text-[10px] text-center text-mictlan-gold mt-2">{feedback}</p>}
+    </div>
+  );
+}

+/* eslint-disable react-refresh/only-export-components */
+import { createContext, useState, useContext } from 'react';
+
+const CardContext = createContext();
+
+export const useCardContext = () => useContext(CardContext);
+
+export const CardProvider = ({ children }) => {
+  const [areCardsOpen, setAreCardsOpen] = useState(true);
+
+  const toggleCards = () => {
+    setAreCardsOpen(prevState => !prevState);
+  };
+
+  return (
+    <CardContext.Provider value={{ areCardsOpen, toggleCards }}>
+      {children}
+    </CardContext.Provider>
+  );
+};

+import { initializeApp, getApps } from "firebase/app";
+    import { getFirestore } from "firebase/firestore";
+import { getAuth } from "firebase/auth";
+
+const firebaseConfig = {
+  apiKey: import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY,
+  authDomain: import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN,
+  projectId: import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID,
+  storageBucket: import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET,
+  messagingSenderId: import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID,
+  appId: import.meta.env.VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID
+};
+
+// Enable Firestore debug logging to diagnose "offline" errors
+// setLogLevel('debug');
+
+// Validate required config
+const requiredFields = ['apiKey', 'authDomain', 'projectId'];
+const missingFields = requiredFields.filter(field => !firebaseConfig[field]);
+
+if (missingFields.length > 0) {
+  console.error('Firebase configuration is incomplete. Missing fields:', missingFields);
+  console.error('Available env vars:', Object.keys(import.meta.env).filter(key => key.startsWith('VITE_FIREBASE_')));
+  throw new Error(`Firebase configuration incomplete. Missing: ${missingFields.join(', ')}`);
+}
+
+// Singleton pattern pour éviter les doubles initialisations (Source: Firebase SDK Best Practices)
+const app = getApps().length === 0 ? initializeApp(firebaseConfig) : getApps()[0];
+
+export const db = getFirestore(app);
+export const auth = getAuth(app);
 export default app;

+import { db } from './config';
+import { collection, getDocs, writeBatch, doc, setDoc, deleteDoc } from 'firebase/firestore'; // Added deleteDoc
+
+export const resetDatabase = async (numberOfTables) => {
+  const count = parseInt(numberOfTables);
+  if (isNaN(count) || count < 1) {
+    alert("Veuillez entrer un nombre valide.");
+    return;
+  }
+  
+  const batch = writeBatch(db);
+  
+  try {
+    // 1. Suppression de l'existant
+    const tablesSnapshot = await getDocs(collection(db, "tables"));
+    tablesSnapshot.docs.forEach((d) => batch.delete(d.ref));
+
+    // 2. Création des nouvelles tables
+    for (let i = 1; i <= count; i++) {
+      // On utilise setDoc via le batch pour s'assurer que le doc est CRÉÉ
+      const tableRef = doc(db, "tables", `table_${i}`);
+      batch.set(tableRef, {
+        id: i,
+        name: `T${i}`,
+        status: "idle",
+        isWaiting: false,
+        needsBill: false,
+        lastUpdate: new Date()
+      });
+    }
+
+    // 3. Réinitialisation de l'inventaire
+    const defaultInventory = [
+      { id: 'tpe_paper', name: 'Rouleaux TPE', status: 'ok', category: 'technique' },
+      { id: 'beer_keg', name: 'Fût de Bière', status: 'ok', category: 'bar' }
+    ];
+
+    defaultInventory.forEach((item) => {
+      const invRef = doc(db, "inventory", item.id);
+      batch.set(invRef, {
+        ...item,
+        lastUpdate: new Date()
+      });
+    });
+
+    await batch.commit();
+    return true;
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Détails de l'erreur Firestore :", error);
+    throw error;
+  }
+};
+
+export const emptyDatabase = async () => {
+  const batch = writeBatch(db);
+  
+  try {
+    // List of known collections to empty
+    const collections = ['tables', 'inventory', 'logs', 'users'];
+    
+    for (const col of collections) {
+      const snapshot = await getDocs(collection(db, col));
+      snapshot.docs.forEach((d) => batch.delete(d.ref));
+    }
+
+    await batch.commit();
+    return true;
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Détails de l'erreur Firestore :", error);
+    throw error;
+  }
+};
+
+export const addInventoryItem = async (name, category, addLog = null, user = null) => {
+  try {
+    const newItemRef = doc(collection(db, "inventory")); // Firestore will auto-generate ID
+    await setDoc(newItemRef, {
+      name: name,
+      category: category,
+      status: 'ok', // Default status
+      lastUpdate: new Date(),
+    });
+
+    if (addLog) {
+      await addLog(`INVENTAIRE : Ajout de l'article "${name}" (Catégorie: ${category})`, 'info', user);
+    }
+    return true;
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur lors de l'ajout de l'article d'inventaire:", error);
+    throw error;
+  }
+};
+
+export const removeInventoryItem = async (itemId, itemName, addLog = null, user = null) => {
+  try {
+    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "inventory", itemId));
+    if (addLog) {
+      await addLog(`INVENTAIRE : Suppression de l'article "${itemName}"`, 'info', user);
+    }
+    return true;
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur lors de la suppression de l'article d'inventaire:", error);
+    throw error;
+  }
 };

+import { db } from './config';
+import { doc, writeBatch } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+export const initializeFirestoreTables = async (numberOfTables = 4) => {
+  const batch = writeBatch(db);
+  
+  for (let i = 1; i <= numberOfTables; i++) {
+    const tableRef = doc(db, "tables", `table_${i}`);
+    batch.set(tableRef, {
+      id: i,
+      name: `Table ${i}`,
+      status: "idle",
+      isWaiting: false,
+      needsBill: false,
+      lastUpdate: new Date()
+    });
+  }
+
+  try {
+    await batch.commit();
+    console.log("✅ Firestore initialisé avec succès !");
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("❌ Erreur lors de l'initialisation :", error);
+  }
 };

+// a future implementation of this function will call the Zenchef and TheFork APIs
+// for now, it returns mock data
+export const getReservations = async (date) => {
+  console.log(`Fetching reservations for ${date.toLocaleDateString()}`);
+
+  try {
+    const response = await fetch(`https://us-central1-dashboard-mictlan-dev.cloudfunctions.net/getZenchefReservations?date=${date.toISOString()}`);

+    console.log('Zenchef API response from Firebase Function:', data);

+      {
+        name: 'Midi',
+        providers: [
+          { name: 'TheFork', reservations: 10, remaining: 5, capacity: 15 },

+        name: 'Soir',
+        providers: [

+        ],
+      },
+    ];
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error('Error fetching from Zenchef API:', error);
+    // Return mock data in case of an error
+    return [
+      {
+        name: 'Midi',
+        providers: [
+          { name: 'TheFork', reservations: 10, remaining: 5, capacity: 15 },
+          { name: 'Zenchef', reservations: 8, remaining: 7, capacity: 15 },
+        ],
+      },
+      {
+        name: 'Soir',
+        providers: [
+          { name: 'TheFork', reservations: 20, remaining: 10, capacity: 30 },
+          { name: 'Zenchef', reservations: 15, remaining: 5, capacity: 20 },
+        ],
+      },
+    ];
+  }
+};

+  return new Promise((resolve) => {

-export const getTableSettings = async () => {

-    if (docSnap.exists()) {

-    } else {
-      const defaultSettings = { inactivityTimeout: 10 };
-      await setDoc(settingsDocRef, defaultSettings);
-      return defaultSettings;
-    }
-  } catch (error) {
-    console.error("Erreur getTableSettings:", error);
-    return { inactivityTimeout: 10 }; // Fallback
-  }

-export const updateTableSettings = async (newSettings) => {
-  try {
-    await setDoc(settingsDocRef, newSettings, { merge: true });

+import { db } from './config';
+import { doc, getDoc, setDoc } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+const settingsDocRef = doc(db, 'settings', 'tables');
+
+export const getTableSettings = async () => {
+  try {
+    const docSnap = await getDoc(settingsDocRef);
+    if (docSnap.exists()) {
+      return docSnap.data();
+    } else {
+      const defaultSettings = { inactivityTimeout: 10 };
+      await setDoc(settingsDocRef, defaultSettings);
+      return defaultSettings;
+    }
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur getTableSettings:", error);
+    return { inactivityTimeout: 10 }; // Fallback
+  }
+};
+
+export const updateTableSettings = async (newSettings) => {
+  try {
+    await setDoc(settingsDocRef, newSettings, { merge: true });
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur updateTableSettings:", error);
+  }
+};

+import { db } from './config';
+import { doc, updateDoc, serverTimestamp } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+// NEW: Gérer les requêtes individuelles (Toggles)
+export const toggleTableRequest = async (fireId, requestKey, currentValue) => {
+  if (!fireId) return;
+  const tableRef = doc(db, 'tables', fireId);
+  try {
+    await updateDoc(tableRef, {
+      [`requests.${requestKey}`]: !currentValue,
+      lastUpdated: serverTimestamp()
+    });
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur toggleTableRequest:", error);
+  }
+};
+
+// NEW: Mettre à jour le statut (Cycle Linéaire)
+export const updateTableStatus = async (fireId, newStatus) => {
+  if (!fireId) return;
+  const tableRef = doc(db, 'tables', fireId);
+  try {
+    await updateDoc(tableRef, {
+      status: newStatus,
+      lastUpdated: serverTimestamp()
+    });
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur updateTableStatus:", error);
+  }
+};
+
+// NEW: Marquer le début de l'occupation
+export const startOccupation = async (fireId) => {
+  if (!fireId) return;
+  const tableRef = doc(db, 'tables', fireId);
+  try {
+    await updateDoc(tableRef, {
+      status: 'occupied',
+      occupiedTimestamp: serverTimestamp(),
+      lastUpdated: serverTimestamp()
+    });
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur startOccupation:", error);
+  }
+};
+
+// NEW: Reset complet de la table
+export const resetTable = async (fireId) => {
+  if (!fireId) return;
+  const tableRef = doc(db, 'tables', fireId);
+  try {
+    await updateDoc(tableRef, {
+      status: 'idle',
+      requests: {
+        needWaiter: false,
+        readyToOrder: false,
+        dontDisturb: false,
+        nextPlate: false,
+        needBill: false,
+        needTPE: false
+      },
+      occupiedTimestamp: null, // Reset occupied timestamp
+      lastUpdated: serverTimestamp()
+    });
+  } catch (error) {
+    console.error("Erreur resetTable:", error);
+  }
 };

+import * as Tone from 'tone';
+import { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react';
+
+let lastSoundPlayedAt = 0; // Timestamp of the last sound played
+
+export function useAudio(tables, inventory = [], settings = {}) {
+  const [isAudioEnabled, setIsAudioEnabled] = useState(false);
+  const prevTableAlertCount = useRef(0);
+  const prevStockAlertCount = useRef(0);
+
+  // Get the rate limit from settings in seconds, default to 5, and convert to ms
+  const soundRateLimit = (settings.soundRateLimit ?? 5) * 1000;
+
+  const initAudio = async () => {
+    await Tone.start();
+    setIsAudioEnabled(true);
+    
+    // Son de démarrage : une montée en tierce (C4 -> E4)
+    const synth = new Tone.Synth().toDestination();
+    synth.triggerAttackRelease("C4", "8n", Tone.now());
+    synth.triggerAttackRelease("E4", "8n", Tone.now() + 0.1);
+  };
+
+  const disableAudio = () => {
+    setIsAudioEnabled(false);
+  };
+
+  const playSoundWithRateLimit = useCallback((soundFunction) => {
+    const now = Date.now();
+    if (now - lastSoundPlayedAt >= soundRateLimit) {
+      lastSoundPlayedAt = now;
+      soundFunction();
+    } else {
+      console.log(`Sound playback rate-limited to ${soundRateLimit / 1000} seconds.`);
+    }
+  }, [soundRateLimit]);
+
+  const playNotificationSound = useCallback((message) => {
+    if (!isAudioEnabled) return;
+    console.log("Notification sound triggered with message:", message); // Log the message
+    playSoundWithRateLimit(() => {
+      const synth = new Tone.Synth().toDestination();
+      synth.volume.value = -12;
+      synth.triggerAttackRelease("A5", "16n");
+    });
+  }, [isAudioEnabled, playSoundWithRateLimit]);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!isAudioEnabled) return;
+
+    // --- 1. SURVEILLANCE DES TABLES ---
+    const currentTableAlerts = tables.filter(t => t.status === 'alert').length;
+    if (currentTableAlerts > prevTableAlertCount.current) {
+      // Son d'alerte salle : Accord cristallin
+      playSoundWithRateLimit(() => {
+        const bell = new Tone.PolySynth(Tone.Synth).toDestination();
+        bell.set({ volume: -10, envelope: { release: 2 } });
+        bell.triggerAttackRelease(["E5", "G5", "B5"], "4n");
+      });
+    }
+    prevTableAlertCount.current = currentTableAlerts;
+
+    // --- 2. SURVEILLANCE DES STOCKS (CRITIQUE) ---
+    const currentStockAlerts = inventory.filter(item => item.status === 'out').length;
+    if (currentStockAlerts > prevStockAlertCount.current) {
+      // Son d'alerte stock : Note plus grave et percutante
+      playSoundWithRateLimit(() => {
+        const synth = new Tone.MembraneSynth().toDestination();
+        synth.volume.value = -5;
+        synth.triggerAttackRelease("G2", "4n");
+      });
+    }
+    prevStockAlertCount.current = currentStockAlerts;
+
+  }, [tables, inventory, isAudioEnabled, playSoundWithRateLimit]);
+
+  return { isAudioEnabled, initAudio, disableAudio, playNotificationSound };
 }

+import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react';
+import { auth, db } from '../firebase/config'; // Importez l'instance déjà configurée
+import { getApp } from "firebase/app";
+import { getFunctions, httpsCallable } from "firebase/functions";
+import { useLogs } from './useLogs';
+import { 
+  onAuthStateChanged, 
+  signInAnonymously, 
+  signInWithEmailAndPassword, 
+  signOut,
+  createUserWithEmailAndPassword
+} from 'firebase/auth';
+import { doc, getDoc, setDoc, updateDoc, query, where, collection, getDocs } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+const app = getApp();
+const functions = getFunctions(app, 'us-central1');
+
+export function useAuth() {
+  const [user, setUser] = useState(null);
+  const [userData, setUserData] = useState(null);
+  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
+  const { addLog } = useLogs();
+  const previousUidRef = useRef(null);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    // Écouteur de session
+    const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (currentUser) => {
+      console.log("🔥 État Auth changé :", currentUser ? "Connecté " + currentUser.uid : "Déconnecté");
+      setUser(currentUser);
+      if (currentUser) {
+        const userDocRef = doc(db, 'users', currentUser.uid);
+        const userDoc = await getDoc(userDocRef);
+        if (userDoc.exists()) {
+          setUserData(userDoc.data());
+        } else {
+          // Check if an existing user with this email is an admin
+          let assignedRole = 'regular';
+          if (currentUser.email) {
+            // Firestore does not allow querying for null or missing fields in 'where' clauses for security rules,
+            // but for data consistency, we assume 'email' exists for non-anonymous users.
+            // Also, we search for 'admin' role specifically.
+            const adminQuery = query(collection(db, 'users'), where('email', '==', currentUser.email), where('role', '==', 'admin'));
+            const adminSnapshot = await getDocs(adminQuery);
+            if (!adminSnapshot.empty) {
+              assignedRole = 'admin';
+            }
+          }
+
+          const defaultData = { role: assignedRole, email: currentUser.email, uid: currentUser.uid };
+          await setDoc(userDocRef, defaultData);
+          setUserData(defaultData);
+        }
+
+        // Log login event (only once per unique uid to avoid duplicates)
+        if (previousUidRef.current !== currentUser.uid) {
+          previousUidRef.current = currentUser.uid;
+          await addLog(`Connexion : ${currentUser.email || 'Anonyme'}`, 'info', currentUser.email || 'Anonyme', 'utilisateurs');
+        }
+      } else {
+        setUserData(null);
+        previousUidRef.current = null;
+      }
+      setLoading(false);
+    });
+    return () => unsubscribe();
+  }, []);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const initializeAdmin = async () => {
+      // Only attempt to initialize if we haven't checked/initialized in this session
+      // to avoid constant console noise and Firebase Auth requests.
+      if (window._adminInitialized) return;
+      window._adminInitialized = true;
+
+      try {
+        const adminEmail = 'admin@example.com';
+        const userCredential = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, adminEmail, '123456');
+        const userDoc = doc(db, 'users', userCredential.user.uid);
+        await setDoc(userDoc, { role: 'admin', email: adminEmail });
+        await signOut(auth);
+      } catch (error) {
+        // 'auth/email-already-in-use' is the standard SDK error code
+        if (error.code === 'auth/email-already-in-use' || error.message?.includes('EMAIL_EXISTS')) {
+          // User exists, ensure their role is admin in Firestore
+          try {
+            const q = query(collection(db, 'users'), where('email', '==', 'admin@example.com'));
+            const querySnapshot = await getDocs(q);
+            if (!querySnapshot.empty) {
+              const userDoc = querySnapshot.docs[0];
+              const data = userDoc.data();
+              if (data.role !== 'admin') {
+                await updateDoc(userDoc.ref, { role: 'admin' });
+              }
+            }
+          } catch (fsError) {
+            console.warn('Admin Firestore check failed (likely permissions):', fsError.message);
+          }
+        } else {
+          // Ignore other errors during auto-init to keep console clean, 
+          // or log them as warnings if they aren't critical.
+          console.debug('Admin auto-init skipped or failed:', error.code);
+        }
+      }
+    };
+    initializeAdmin();
+  }, []);
+
+    const loginAnonymously = useCallback(async () => {
+    try {
+      setLoading(true);
+      await signInAnonymously(auth);
+    } catch (err) {
+      console.error("❌ Échec Anonyme :", err.code, err.message);
+    } finally {
+      setLoading(false);
+    }
+  }, []);
+
+  const loginEmail = useCallback(async (email, password) => {
+    try {
+      setLoading(true);
+      await signInWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
+    } catch (err) {
+      console.error("❌ Échec Email :", err.code, err.message);
+      throw err;
+    } finally {
+      setLoading(false);
+    }
+  }, []);
+
+  const logout = useCallback(async () => {
+    const currentUser = auth.currentUser;
+    if (currentUser) {
+      await addLog(`Déconnexion : ${currentUser.email || 'Anonyme'}`, 'info', currentUser.email || 'Anonyme', 'utilisateurs');
+    }
+    return signOut(auth);
+  }, [addLog]);
+
+  const getAllUsers = useCallback(async () => {
+    try {
+      const usersCollection = collection(db, 'users');
+      const userSnapshot = await getDocs(usersCollection);
+      const userList = userSnapshot.docs.map(doc => ({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
+      return userList;
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error("Error fetching users:", error);
+      return [];
+    }
+  }, []);
+
+  const updateUserRole = useCallback(async (userId, newRole) => {
+    try {
+      const userDocRef = doc(db, 'users', userId);
+      await updateDoc(userDocRef, { role: newRole });
+      await addLog(`Rôle modifié : ${userId} → ${newRole}`, 'info', null, 'utilisateurs');
+      return true;
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error("Error updating user role:", error);
+      return false;
+    }
+  }, [addLog]);
+
+  const registerUser = useCallback(async (email, password) => {
+    try {
+      const userCredential = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
+      const user = userCredential.user;
+      await setDoc(doc(db, 'users', user.uid), {
+        uid: user.uid,
+        email: user.email,
+        role: 'regular'
+      });
+      await addLog(`Nouvel utilisateur créé : ${user.email}`, 'info', user.email, 'utilisateurs');
+      return { success: true, user };
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error("Error creating user:", error);
+      return { success: false, error };
+    }
+  }, [addLog]);
+
+  const deleteUser = useCallback(async (uid) => {
+    try {
+      const deleteUserFunction = httpsCallable(functions, 'deleteUser');
+      await deleteUserFunction({ uid });
+      await addLog(`Utilisateur supprimé : ${uid}`, 'warning', null, 'utilisateurs');
+      return { success: true };
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error("Error deleting user:", error);
+      return { success: false, error };
+    }
+  }, [addLog]);
+
+  const changeUserPassword = useCallback(async (uid, newPassword) => {
+    try {
+      const changeUserPasswordFunction = httpsCallable(functions, 'updateUserPassword');
+      await changeUserPasswordFunction({ uid, newPassword });
+      return { success: true };
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error("Error changing user password:", error);
+      return { success: false, error };
+    }
+  }, []);
+
+  return { user, userData, loading, loginAnonymously, loginEmail, logout, getAllUsers, updateUserRole, registerUser, deleteUser, changeUserPassword };
 }

-export function useDishes() {

-    const q = collection(db, "dishes");
-    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(q, (snapshot) => {

-        id: doc.id,
-        ...doc.data()
-      }));

+import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import { db } from '../firebase/config';
+import { collection, onSnapshot } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+export function useDishes() {
+  const [dishes, setDishes] = useState([]);
+  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const q = collection(db, "dishes");
+    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(q, (snapshot) => {
+      const dishesData = snapshot.docs.map(doc => ({
+        id: doc.id,
+        ...doc.data()
+      }));
+      
+      setDishes(dishesData);
+      setLoading(false);
+    }, (error) => {
+      console.error("Erreur Firestore :", error);
+      setLoading(false);
+    });
+
+    return () => unsubscribe();
+  }, []);
+
+  return { dishes, loading };
+}

+import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import { doc, onSnapshot, setDoc, updateDoc } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+const DB_PATH = {
+  STATUS: 'mictlan_dashboard/status',
+};
+
+// Ajoutez "user" comme paramètre pour savoir quand la connexion est prête
+export function useFirestore(db, user) {
+  const [statusData, setStatusData] = useState({});
+  const [tableRequests, setTableRequests] = useState({});
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    // On attend d'avoir la DB ET l'utilisateur connecté avant d'écouter
+    if (!db || !user) return;
+
+    const docRef = doc(db, DB_PATH.STATUS);
+
+    const unsubStatus = onSnapshot(docRef, 
+      (docSnap) => {
+        if (docSnap.exists()) {
+          const data = docSnap.data();
+          setStatusData(data.inventory || {});
+          setTableRequests(data.tableRequests || {});
+        } else {
+          // Créer le document s'il n'existe pas (initialisation)
+          setDoc(docRef, { inventory: {}, tableRequests: {} }, { merge: true });
+        }
+      },
+      (error) => {
+        console.error("Erreur Firestore (Permissions ?):", error);
+      }
+    );
+
+    return () => {
+      unsubStatus();
+    };
+  }, [db, user]); // On re-exécute quand l'user change
+
+  const updateInventoryStatus = async (itemKey, newStatus) => {
+    if (!user) return;
+    const statusRef = doc(db, DB_PATH.STATUS);
+    await setDoc(statusRef, { 
+      inventory: { [itemKey]: newStatus } 
+    }, { merge: true });
+  };
+
+  const updateTableRequest = async (tableId, requestType, value) => {
+    if (!user) return;
+    const statusRef = doc(db, DB_PATH.STATUS);
+    const fieldPath = `tableRequests.${tableId}.${requestType}`;
+    await updateDoc(statusRef, {
+      [fieldPath]: value
+    });
+  };
+
+  return { statusData, tableRequests, updateInventoryStatus, updateTableRequest };
 }

+import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import { db } from '../firebase/config';
+import { collection, onSnapshot, doc, updateDoc } from 'firebase/firestore';
+import { addInventoryItem, removeInventoryItem } from '../firebase/dbActions'; // Import addInventoryItem and removeInventoryItem
+
+export function useInventory() {
+  const [inventory, setInventory] = useState([]);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(collection(db, "inventory"), (snapshot) => {
+      const data = snapshot.docs.map(doc => ({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
+      setInventory(data);
+    });
+    return () => unsubscribe();
+  }, []);
+
+  const updateStatus = async (itemId, nextStatus, itemName, addLog, user = null) => {
+    try {
+      const itemRef = doc(db, "inventory", itemId);
+      await updateDoc(itemRef, { 
+        status: nextStatus,
+        lastUpdate: new Date() 
+      });
+
+      const statusLabels = { ok: 'EN STOCK', low: 'BAS', out: 'VIDE/CRITIQUE' };
+      
+      if (addLog) {
+        await addLog(`STOCK : ${itemName} est passé en [${statusLabels[nextStatus]}]`, 'info', user);
+      }
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error("Erreur mise à jour inventaire:", error);
+    }
+  };
+
+  return { inventory, updateStatus, addInventoryItem, removeInventoryItem };
 }

-export function useLogs() {

-      setLogs(data);
-    });

-  }, []);

-    try {
-      await addDoc(collection(db, "logs"), {
-        message,

+import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import { db } from '../firebase/config';
+import { collection, query, orderBy, limit, onSnapshot, addDoc, serverTimestamp } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+export function useLogs() {
+  const [logs, setLogs] = useState([]);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const q = query(collection(db, "logs"), orderBy("timestamp", "desc"), limit(500));
+    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(q, (snapshot) => {
+      const data = snapshot.docs.map(doc => ({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
+      setLogs(data);
+    });
+    return () => unsubscribe();
+  }, []);
+
+  const addLog = async (message, type = 'info', user = null, category = 'inventaire') => {
+    try {
+      await addDoc(collection(db, "logs"), {
+        message,
+        type,
+        category,
+        user: user ? (user.isAnonymous ? 'Anonyme' : user.email) : null,
+        timestamp: serverTimestamp()
+      });
+    } catch (err) {
+      console.error("Erreur log:", err);
+    }
+  };
+
+  return { logs, addLog };
+}

-  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);

-        setSettings(prevSettings => ({ ...prevSettings, ...dbSettings }));
-

-          localStorage.setItem('anonymousLoginEnabled', JSON.stringify(dbSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled));
-        }
-      } else {

-          setSettings(prevSettings => ({ ...prevSettings, ...defaultSettings }));
-          if (typeof defaultSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled === 'boolean') {

-  }, []);
-
-  const updateSettings = useCallback(async (newSettings) => {
-    // Update local state and localStorage immediately for instant UI feedback and persistence
-    setSettings(prevSettings => ({ ...prevSettings, ...newSettings }));
-    if (typeof newSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled === 'boolean') {
-      localStorage.setItem('anonymousLoginEnabled', JSON.stringify(newSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled));
-    }
-

-    await updateTableSettings(newSettings);
-  }, []);

+import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
+import { getTableSettings, updateTableSettings } from '../firebase/settingsActions';
+import { db } from '../firebase/config';
+import { doc, onSnapshot } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+// Helper to get initial settings from localStorage or set defaults
+const getInitialSettings = () => {
+  const storedAnonymous = localStorage.getItem('anonymousLoginEnabled');
+  return {
+    inactivityTimeout: 10,
+    anonymousLoginEnabled: storedAnonymous !== null ? JSON.parse(storedAnonymous) : true,
+  };
+};
+
+export function useSettings() {
+  const [settings, setSettings] = useState(getInitialSettings);
+  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const settingsDocRef = doc(db, 'settings', 'tables');
+    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(settingsDocRef, (docSnap) => {
+      if (docSnap.exists()) {
+        const dbSettings = docSnap.data();
+        setSettings(prevSettings => ({ ...prevSettings, ...dbSettings }));
+        
+        if (typeof dbSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled === 'boolean') {
+          localStorage.setItem('anonymousLoginEnabled', JSON.stringify(dbSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled));
+        }
+      } else {
+        getTableSettings().then(defaultSettings => {
+          setSettings(prevSettings => ({ ...prevSettings, ...defaultSettings }));
+          if (typeof defaultSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled === 'boolean') {
+            localStorage.setItem('anonymousLoginEnabled', JSON.stringify(defaultSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled));
+          }
+        });
+      }
+      setLoading(false);
+    }, (error) => {
+      console.error("Erreur listening to settings:", error);
+      setLoading(false); // Fallback to initial state from localStorage
+    });
+
+    return () => unsubscribe();
+  }, []);
+
+  const updateSettings = useCallback(async (newSettings) => {
+    // Update local state and localStorage immediately for instant UI feedback and persistence
+    setSettings(prevSettings => ({ ...prevSettings, ...newSettings }));
+    if (typeof newSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled === 'boolean') {
+      localStorage.setItem('anonymousLoginEnabled', JSON.stringify(newSettings.anonymousLoginEnabled));
+    }
+    
+    // Then, update Firestore
+    await updateTableSettings(newSettings);
+  }, []);
+
+  return { settings, loading, updateSettings };
+}

-import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
-import { db } from '../firebase/config';

-export function useTables() {

-    //const q = query(collection(db, "tables"), orderBy("id", "asc"));
-    const q = collection(db, "tables");
-    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(q, (snapshot) => {
-      const tablesData = snapshot.docs.map(doc => ({
-        fireId: doc.id, // l'ID du document Firestore
-        ...doc.data()   // les champs (status, type, etc.)
-      }));
-

-      setLoading(false);
-    }, (error) => {
-      console.error("Erreur Firestore :", error);
-      setLoading(false);

+import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
+import { db } from '../firebase/config';
+import { collection, onSnapshot } from 'firebase/firestore';
+
+export function useTables() {
+  const [tables, setTables] = useState([]);
+  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    // On cible la collection "tables" (vérifiez que c'est bien le nom dans Firestore)
+    //const q = query(collection(db, "tables"), orderBy("id", "asc"));
+    const q = collection(db, "tables");
+    const unsubscribe = onSnapshot(q, (snapshot) => {
+      const tablesData = snapshot.docs.map(doc => ({
+        fireId: doc.id, // l'ID du document Firestore
+        ...doc.data()   // les champs (status, type, etc.)
+      }));
+      
+      setTables(tablesData);
+      setLoading(false);
+    }, (error) => {
+      console.error("Erreur Firestore :", error);
+      setLoading(false);
+    });
+
+    return () => unsubscribe(); // Nettoyage du listener
+  }, []);
+
+  return { tables, loading };
 }

-my unsplash developer access key is 5zrLJxGBNc6dn288gm2QyNiQ7iRhqO10QK7Cm1hFCjg
-
-Since the Unsplash API returns **JSON** (a structured text format) instead of a direct image file, you need to "parse" that data to find the specific image URL you want to display.
-
-Think of the JSON as a delivery manifest: it tells you the photographer's name, the photo description, and—most importantly—a list of links to the image in different sizes.
-
-### 1. Identify the URL you need
-
-Inside the JSON response, look for the `"urls"` object. It usually looks like this:
-
-```json
-"urls": {
-  "raw": "...",
-  "full": "...",
-  "regular": "...",
-  "small": "...",
-  "thumb": "..."
-}
-
-```
-
-* **Small/Thumb:** Best for profile pictures or grid layouts.
-* **Regular:** Best for blog posts or standard website content.
-* **Full/Raw:** Only use these if the user needs to download a high-res version (they are very large files).
-
----
-
-### 2. Use it in HTML/JavaScript (Frontend)
-
-If you are building a website, you use `fetch()` to get the data, then inject the URL into an `<img>` tag.
-
-**Example Code:**
-
-```javascript
-async function getPhoto() {
-  const response = await fetch('https://api.unsplash.com/photos/random?client_id=YOUR_ACCESS_KEY');
-  const data = await response.json();
-
-  // 1. Grab the 'regular' size URL from the JSON
-  const imageUrl = data.urls.regular;
-
-  // 2. Put it into an <img> tag on your page
-  document.getElementById('myImage').src = imageUrl;
-}
-
-```
-
----
-
-### 3. Use it in Python (Backend/Data)
-
-If you are using Python for a bot or data project, you use the `requests` library to "dig" into the dictionary.
-
-**Example Code:**
-
-```python
-import requests
-
-url = "https://api.unsplash.com/photos/random?client_id=YOUR_ACCESS_KEY"
-response = requests.get(url)
-data = response.json()
-
-# Accessing the nested data
-photo_url = data['urls']['regular']
-photographer = data['user']['name']
-
-print(f"This photo was taken by {photographer}: {photo_url}")
-
-```
-
----
-
-### 4. Important: Don't forget Attribution
-
-Unsplash requires you to credit the photographer. The JSON data provides everything you need for this. You should look for:
-
-* `data.user.name` (The person to credit)
-* `data.user.links.html` (The link to their profile)
-
-**A standard credit looks like:**
-
-> Photo by [Photographer Name] on [Unsplash]
-
----
-
-### Summary Table
-
-| If you want to... | Look for this in the JSON |
-| --- | --- |
-| **Show the image** | `urls.regular` |
-| **Give credit** | `user.name` and `user.links.html` |
-| **Show a caption** | `description` or `alt_description` |
-| **Track a download** | `links.download_location` (Required by Unsplash for downloads) |
-
+my unsplash developer access key is 5zrLJxGBNc6dn288gm2QyNiQ7iRhqO10QK7Cm1hFCjg
+
+Since the Unsplash API returns **JSON** (a structured text format) instead of a direct image file, you need to "parse" that data to find the specific image URL you want to display.
+
+Think of the JSON as a delivery manifest: it tells you the photographer's name, the photo description, and—most importantly—a list of links to the image in different sizes.
+
+### 1. Identify the URL you need
+
+Inside the JSON response, look for the `"urls"` object. It usually looks like this:
+
+```json
+"urls": {
+  "raw": "...",
+  "full": "...",
+  "regular": "...",
+  "small": "...",
+  "thumb": "..."
+}
+
+```
+
+* **Small/Thumb:** Best for profile pictures or grid layouts.
+* **Regular:** Best for blog posts or standard website content.
+* **Full/Raw:** Only use these if the user needs to download a high-res version (they are very large files).
+
+---
+
+### 2. Use it in HTML/JavaScript (Frontend)
+
+If you are building a website, you use `fetch()` to get the data, then inject the URL into an `<img>` tag.
+
+**Example Code:**
+
+```javascript
+async function getPhoto() {
+  const response = await fetch('https://api.unsplash.com/photos/random?client_id=YOUR_ACCESS_KEY');
+  const data = await response.json();
+
+  // 1. Grab the 'regular' size URL from the JSON
+  const imageUrl = data.urls.regular;
+
+  // 2. Put it into an <img> tag on your page
+  document.getElementById('myImage').src = imageUrl;
+}
+
+```
+
+---
+
+### 3. Use it in Python (Backend/Data)
+
+If you are using Python for a bot or data project, you use the `requests` library to "dig" into the dictionary.
+
+**Example Code:**
+
+```python
+import requests
+
+url = "https://api.unsplash.com/photos/random?client_id=YOUR_ACCESS_KEY"
+response = requests.get(url)
+data = response.json()
+
+# Accessing the nested data
+photo_url = data['urls']['regular']
+photographer = data['user']['name']
+
+print(f"This photo was taken by {photographer}: {photo_url}")
+
+```
+
+---
+
+### 4. Important: Don't forget Attribution
+
+Unsplash requires you to credit the photographer. The JSON data provides everything you need for this. You should look for:
+
+* `data.user.name` (The person to credit)
+* `data.user.links.html` (The link to their profile)
+
+**A standard credit looks like:**
+
+> Photo by [Photographer Name] on [Unsplash]
+
+---
+
+### Summary Table
+
+| If you want to... | Look for this in the JSON |
+| --- | --- |
+| **Show the image** | `urls.regular` |
+| **Give credit** | `user.name` and `user.links.html` |
+| **Show a caption** | `description` or `alt_description` |
+| **Track a download** | `links.download_location` (Required by Unsplash for downloads) |
+
 Would you like me to create a complete, clickable HTML file for you that fetches and displays a random yogurt image using your key?

-# Detailed Prompt to Recreate the Mictlan Dashboard Application
-
-The following prompt provides a comprehensive guide to recreate the Mictlan Dashboard application, a web-based dashboard for managing a restaurant's operations.
-
-## 1. Project Overview
-
-The Mictlan Dashboard is a real-time, interactive application designed for a restaurant named "Mictlan". It provides a centralized interface for restaurant staff to monitor and manage table statuses, customer requests, inventory, and other operational tasks. The application is designed to be used on a phone, tablet or a desktop computer.
-
-**Key Features:**
-
-*   Real-time table management with status updates and customer requests.
-*   Two distinct view modes for table management: a simple "linear cycle" mode and a detailed "toggles" mode.
-*   Inventory tracking and management.
-*   Product management with features for adding, listing, and reporting on products.
-*   Admin panel for managing application settings and data.
-*   User authentication with different roles (e.g., admin, user).
-*   Audio notifications for important events.
-*   A cleaning checklist for staff.
-
-## 2. Technology Stack
-
-*   **Frontend:**
-    *   **Framework:** React
-    *   **Build Tool:** Vite
-    *   **Styling:** Tailwind CSS
-    *   **Routing:** `react-router-dom`
-    *   **Icons:** `lucide-react`
-*   **Backend:**
-    *   **Platform:** Firebase
-    *   **Database:** Firestore
-    *   **Authentication:** Firebase Authentication
-    *   **Serverless Functions:** Firebase Cloud Functions
-
-## 3. Firebase Setup
-
-### 3.1. Firestore Database
-
-Create the following collections in your Firestore database:
-
-*   **`tables`**:
-    *   **Document ID:** Auto-generated
-    *   **Fields:**
-        *   `id` (Number): The table number.
-        *   `name` (String): The table name (e.g., "T1").
-        *   `status` (String): The current status of the table (e.g., "idle", "occupied", "alert", "cleaning", "tout va bien?").
-        *   `requests` (Map): A map of customer requests.
-            *   `needWaiter` (Boolean)
-            *   `readyToOrder` (Boolean)
-            *   `dontDisturb` (Boolean)
-            *   `nextPlate` (Boolean)
-            *   `needBill` (Boolean)
-            *   `needTPE` (Boolean)
-        *   `lastUpdate` (Timestamp): The timestamp of the last update.
-        *   `occupiedTimestamp` (Timestamp): The timestamp when the table was occupied.
-*   **`inventory`**:
-    *   **Document ID:** Auto-generated
-    *   **Fields:**
-        *   `name` (String): The name of the inventory item.
-        *   `category` (String): The category of the item (e.g., "technique", "bar").
-        *   `status` (String): The status of the item (e.g., "ok", "low", "out").
-        *   `lastUpdate` (Timestamp): The timestamp of the last update.
-*   **`products`**:
-    *   **Document ID:** Auto-generated
-    *   **Fields:**
-        *   `name` (String): The name of the product.
-        *   `weight` (Number): The weight of the product.
-        *   `unit` (String): The unit of measurement (e.g., "g", "ml", "kg").
-        *   `expiryDate` (Timestamp): The expiration date of the product.
-        *   `quantity` (Number): The quantity of the product.
-        *   `category` (String): The category of the product (e.g., "Laitier", "Traiteur", "Boissons").
-        *   `price` (Number): The price of the product.
-        *   `photos` (Array of Strings): An array of URLs to product photos.
-        *   `decisionStatus` (String): The status of the decision (e.g., "to_handle", "promoted", "donated").
-        *   `creationDate` (Timestamp): The timestamp of the product's creation.
-*   **`users`**:
-    *   **Document ID:** User's UID from Firebase Authentication.
-    *   **Fields:**
-        *   `email` (String): The user's email address.
-        *   `role` (String): The user's role (e.g., "admin", "user").
-
-### 3.2. Firebase Authentication
-
-Enable Email/Password and Anonymous authentication in the Firebase console.
-
-### 3.3. Firebase Cloud Functions
-
-Create the following Cloud Functions:
-
-*   **`populateProducts`**:
-    *   **Trigger:** HTTPS (onCall)
-    *   **Logic:** Populates the `products` collection with a predefined list of test products.
-*   **`clearProducts`**:
-    *   **Trigger:** HTTPS (onCall)
-    *   **Logic:** Deletes all documents from the `products` collection.
-
-## 4. Component Breakdown
-
-Create the following React components in the `src/components` directory:
-
-*   **`App.jsx`**: The main component that sets up the application layout, routing, and provides context to other components.
-*   **`Header.jsx`**: The application header, which displays the app title, user information, and a logout button.
-*   **`Login.jsx`**: The login page, with forms for email/password and anonymous login.
-*   **`TableGrid.jsx`**: The core component for managing tables. It should implement the two view modes ("linear" and "toggles") and the logic for updating table statuses and requests.
-*   **`StatusDashboard.jsx`**: A dashboard component to display the overall status of the restaurant.
-*   **`Inventory.jsx`**: A component to display and manage inventory items.
-*   **`ShoppingList.jsx`**: A component for managing a shopping list.
-*   **`ActivityLog.jsx`**: A component to display a log of activities.
-*   **`AdminPanel.jsx`**: A panel for administrators to manage application settings and data.
-*   **`Manual.jsx`**: A component that displays a manual for using the application.
-*   **`CleaningChecklist.jsx`**: A checklist for cleaning tasks.
-*   **`ProductForm.jsx`, `ProductList.jsx`, `ProductDecisions.jsx`, `ProductReporting.jsx`**: Components for managing products.
-*   **`ClientSuggestions.jsx`**: A component to display client suggestions.
-*   **`FooterLinks.jsx`**: The application footer, with links to social media and other resources.
-*   **`Card.jsx`**: A reusable card component for displaying content in a consistent style.
-
-## 5. Hooks
-
-Create the following custom hooks in the `src/hooks` directory:
-
-*   **`useAuth.js`**: A hook for managing user authentication state.
-*   **`useTables.js`**: A hook for fetching and managing table data from Firestore.
-*   **`useInventory.js`**: A hook for fetching and managing inventory data from Firestore.
-*   **`useSettings.js`**: A hook for managing application settings.
-*   **`useAudio.js`**: A hook for playing audio notifications.
-
-## 6. User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX)
-
-*   **Theme:** The application uses a dark theme with a color palette inspired by "Mictlan", the Aztec underworld.
-    *   **Primary Background:** `#0a0a0a` (a very dark gray)
-    *   **Primary Text:** `#d4af37` (a golden color)
-    *   **Accent Colors:** Use shades of red, orange, green, blue, and purple for status indicators and buttons.
-*   **Layout:** The application should have a responsive layout that works well on both desktop and tablet and phone devices. The main content area should be a grid of cards, each displaying a different module (e.g., `TableGrid`, `Inventory`).
-*   **Typography:** Use a clean, modern, and readable font.
-
-By following this prompt, an AI model should be able to recreate the Mictlan Dashboard application with all its key features and functionalities.
+# Detailed Prompt to Recreate the Mictlan Dashboard Application
+
+The following prompt provides a comprehensive guide to recreate the Mictlan Dashboard application, a web-based dashboard for managing a restaurant's operations.
+
+## 1. Project Overview
+
+The Mictlan Dashboard is a real-time, interactive application designed for a restaurant named "Mictlan". It provides a centralized interface for restaurant staff to monitor and manage table statuses, customer requests, inventory, and other operational tasks. The application is designed to be used on a phone, tablet or a desktop computer.
+
+**Key Features:**
+
+*   Real-time table management with status updates and customer requests.
+*   Two distinct view modes for table management: a simple "linear cycle" mode and a detailed "toggles" mode.
+*   Inventory tracking and management.
+*   Product management with features for adding, listing, and reporting on products.
+*   Admin panel for managing application settings and data.
+*   User authentication with different roles (e.g., admin, user).
+*   Audio notifications for important events.
+*   A cleaning checklist for staff.
+
+## 2. Technology Stack
+
+*   **Frontend:**
+    *   **Framework:** React
+    *   **Build Tool:** Vite
+    *   **Styling:** Tailwind CSS
+    *   **Routing:** `react-router-dom`
+    *   **Icons:** `lucide-react`
+*   **Backend:**
+    *   **Platform:** Firebase
+    *   **Database:** Firestore
+    *   **Authentication:** Firebase Authentication
+    *   **Serverless Functions:** Firebase Cloud Functions
+
+## 3. Firebase Setup
+
+### 3.1. Firestore Database
+
+Create the following collections in your Firestore database:
+
+*   **`tables`**:
+    *   **Document ID:** Auto-generated
+    *   **Fields:**
+        *   `id` (Number): The table number.
+        *   `name` (String): The table name (e.g., "T1").
+        *   `status` (String): The current status of the table (e.g., "idle", "occupied", "alert", "cleaning", "tout va bien?").
+        *   `requests` (Map): A map of customer requests.
+            *   `needWaiter` (Boolean)
+            *   `readyToOrder` (Boolean)
+            *   `dontDisturb` (Boolean)
+            *   `nextPlate` (Boolean)
+            *   `needBill` (Boolean)
+            *   `needTPE` (Boolean)
+        *   `lastUpdate` (Timestamp): The timestamp of the last update.
+        *   `occupiedTimestamp` (Timestamp): The timestamp when the table was occupied.
+*   **`inventory`**:
+    *   **Document ID:** Auto-generated
+    *   **Fields:**
+        *   `name` (String): The name of the inventory item.
+        *   `category` (String): The category of the item (e.g., "technique", "bar").
+        *   `status` (String): The status of the item (e.g., "ok", "low", "out").
+        *   `lastUpdate` (Timestamp): The timestamp of the last update.
+*   **`products`**:
+    *   **Document ID:** Auto-generated
+    *   **Fields:**
+        *   `name` (String): The name of the product.
+        *   `weight` (Number): The weight of the product.
+        *   `unit` (String): The unit of measurement (e.g., "g", "ml", "kg").
+        *   `expiryDate` (Timestamp): The expiration date of the product.
+        *   `quantity` (Number): The quantity of the product.
+        *   `category` (String): The category of the product (e.g., "Laitier", "Traiteur", "Boissons").
+        *   `price` (Number): The price of the product.
+        *   `photos` (Array of Strings): An array of URLs to product photos.
+        *   `decisionStatus` (String): The status of the decision (e.g., "to_handle", "promoted", "donated").
+        *   `creationDate` (Timestamp): The timestamp of the product's creation.
+*   **`users`**:
+    *   **Document ID:** User's UID from Firebase Authentication.
+    *   **Fields:**
+        *   `email` (String): The user's email address.
+        *   `role` (String): The user's role (e.g., "admin", "user").
+
+### 3.2. Firebase Authentication
+
+Enable Email/Password and Anonymous authentication in the Firebase console.
+
+### 3.3. Firebase Cloud Functions
+
+Create the following Cloud Functions:
+
+*   **`populateProducts`**:
+    *   **Trigger:** HTTPS (onCall)
+    *   **Logic:** Populates the `products` collection with a predefined list of test products.
+*   **`clearProducts`**:
+    *   **Trigger:** HTTPS (onCall)
+    *   **Logic:** Deletes all documents from the `products` collection.
+
+## 4. Component Breakdown
+
+Create the following React components in the `src/components` directory:
+
+*   **`App.jsx`**: The main component that sets up the application layout, routing, and provides context to other components.
+*   **`Header.jsx`**: The application header, which displays the app title, user information, and a logout button.
+*   **`Login.jsx`**: The login page, with forms for email/password and anonymous login.
+*   **`TableGrid.jsx`**: The core component for managing tables. It should implement the two view modes ("linear" and "toggles") and the logic for updating table statuses and requests.
+*   **`StatusDashboard.jsx`**: A dashboard component to display the overall status of the restaurant.
+*   **`Inventory.jsx`**: A component to display and manage inventory items.
+*   **`ShoppingList.jsx`**: A component for managing a shopping list.
+*   **`ActivityLog.jsx`**: A component to display a log of activities.
+*   **`AdminPanel.jsx`**: A panel for administrators to manage application settings and data.
+*   **`Manual.jsx`**: A component that displays a manual for using the application.
+*   **`CleaningChecklist.jsx`**: A checklist for cleaning tasks.
+*   **`ProductForm.jsx`, `ProductList.jsx`, `ProductDecisions.jsx`, `ProductReporting.jsx`**: Components for managing products.
+*   **`ClientSuggestions.jsx`**: A component to display client suggestions.
+*   **`FooterLinks.jsx`**: The application footer, with links to social media and other resources.
+*   **`Card.jsx`**: A reusable card component for displaying content in a consistent style.
+
+## 5. Hooks
+
+Create the following custom hooks in the `src/hooks` directory:
+
+*   **`useAuth.js`**: A hook for managing user authentication state.
+*   **`useTables.js`**: A hook for fetching and managing table data from Firestore.
+*   **`useInventory.js`**: A hook for fetching and managing inventory data from Firestore.
+*   **`useSettings.js`**: A hook for managing application settings.
+*   **`useAudio.js`**: A hook for playing audio notifications.
+
+## 6. User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX)
+
+*   **Theme:** The application uses a dark theme with a color palette inspired by "Mictlan", the Aztec underworld.
+    *   **Primary Background:** `#0a0a0a` (a very dark gray)
+    *   **Primary Text:** `#d4af37` (a golden color)
+    *   **Accent Colors:** Use shades of red, orange, green, blue, and purple for status indicators and buttons.
+*   **Layout:** The application should have a responsive layout that works well on both desktop and tablet and phone devices. The main content area should be a grid of cards, each displaying a different module (e.g., `TableGrid`, `Inventory`).
+*   **Typography:** Use a clean, modern, and readable font.
+
+By following this prompt, an AI model should be able to recreate the Mictlan Dashboard application with all its key features and functionalities.

-    *   This step ensures the GitHub Actions service account has all necessary permissions for a successful production deployment. The service account is typically identified by an email like `github-actions-deployer@<your-project-id>.iam.gserviceaccount.com`.
-    *   **Action:** Provide your Firebase production project ID and the service account email when prompted. The agent will then generate and ask you to execute `gcloud` commands to grant the required IAM roles.
-    *   **Required Roles:**
-        *   `Firebase Hosting Admin` (`roles/firebasehosting.admin`) - For deploying hosting artifacts.
-        *   `Firebase Extensions Admin` (`roles/firebaseextensions.admin`) - For managing Firebase Extensions (e.g., when deploying functions that interact with extensions).
-        *   `Cloud Functions Viewer` (`roles/cloudfunctions.viewer`) - For listing Cloud Functions.
-        *   `Cloud Functions Developer` (`roles/cloudfunctions.developer`) - For creating, updating, and deploying Cloud Functions.
-        *   `Cloud Functions Admin` (`roles/cloudfunctions.admin`) - **Required when deploying new HTTP (`onRequest`) functions** (needed for `setIamPolicy` to make them publicly accessible). Without this, deployment fails with "Missing required permission: cloudfunctions.functions.setIamPolicy". Grant with: `gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding <prod-project-id> --member="serviceAccount:<sa-email>" --role="roles/cloudfunctions.admin"`

-    *   **Loop and poll:** Implement a loop that polls `gh run list --branch <branch-name> --json status,conclusion,name,databaseId,url --limit 5` every 30 seconds for up to 10 minutes (20 retries).
-    *   **Filter and check status:** Use `jq` to filter the JSON output to find the run corresponding to the "CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)" workflow (or similar). Wait until its `status` is "completed" and `conclusion` is "success".
-    *   **Timeout/Failure Handling:** If the timeout is reached or conclusion is 'failure', report it to the user, provide a link to the workflow run, and ask if they want to:
-        1.  Re-run the workflow (after addressing potential issues).
-        2.  Proceed with the release manually (skipping automated checks).
-    *   **Get Run ID:** Extract the `databaseId` of the successful run.

Search for a line containing `"url":` and `web.app` — the URL will be masked in the logs (project ID replaced by `***`). **Reconstruct the real URL** by replacing `***` with the `prod` project ID read from `.firebaserc` (key `"prod"`). The final URL follows this pattern: `https://<prod-project-id>--pr-<pr-number>-<random-string>.web.app`. Example: if `.firebaserc` has `"prod": "dashboard-mictlan-prod"` and the masked URL is `https://***--pr-21-v23yvsa9.web.app`, the real URL is `https://dashboard-mictlan-prod--pr-21-v23yvsa9.web.app`.

-# Liste de choses à faire
-
-> ⚠️ **Sécurité** : Token API Zenchef et Restaurant ID à stocker dans les variables d'environnement (ne pas committer en clair).
-
----
-
-## Fixes
-
-- [ ] **Multi-device login** : Se connecter avec un même compte depuis un appareil différent crée un second utilisateur en base sans droits admin. Il faut accorder le même rôle au nouveau UID.
-
-- [ ] **Tâche expirée** : Quand une tâche expire automatiquement, la barre passe au vert, affiche "Il reste 30 secondes" et la date du prochain nettoyage change toutes les secondes.
-
----
-
-## Gestion des Stocks et des Dates d'Expiration
-
-- [x] **Plats** : Lister les plats, afficher leur composition (ingrédients) et pouvoir en rajouter. *(DishList + DishForm implémentés dans InventoryManagementDashboard)*
-
-- [x] **Suggestions clients — Seuil d'expiration** : Nouveau champ pour changer le nombre de jours avant expiration, avec placeholder explicatif quand vide. *(Input + icône HelpCircle dans ClientSuggestions.jsx)*
-
-- [x] **Affichage des images produits** : Les images ajoutées aux produits ne s'affichaient pas. *(Upload Firebase Storage + affichage dans ProductList.jsx — les anciennes données de test avaient des liens Unsplash factices, le code est correct)*
-
-- [x] **Pages dédiées** : Page séparée pour les produits/plats, et page séparée pour les tâches de nettoyage. *(Routes `/products` et `/cleaning-tasks` dans App.jsx)*
-
-- [x] **Suppression des suggestions après décision** : Quand une décision est prise (Soldé / Donné / Jeté), le produit disparaît de la liste des suggestions. *(ClientSuggestions.jsx filtre sur `decisionStatus === 'to_handle'`)*
-
-- [x] **Données de test protégées** : Effacer les données de test ne supprime pas les données saisies manuellement. Les données de test incluent le préfixe "TEST -" et le flag `isTestData: true`. *(Cloud Functions clearProducts/clearDishes filtrent sur `isTestData === true`)*
-
-- [x] **Autocomplétion à la création** :
-  - [x] `ProductForm.jsx` — Ajouter `<datalist>` sur le champ nom via `useProducts`
-  - [x] `ProductForm.jsx` — Préremplir poids, unité, quantité, catégorie, prix quand un nom existant est sélectionné (sauf date de péremption)
-  - [x] `ProductForm.jsx` — Logger la création du produit (`addLog`, catégorie `'inventory'`)
-
-- [x] **Filtrage de la liste des produits** :
-  - [x] `ProductList.jsx` — Mode par défaut : afficher uniquement les `to_handle` + les autres avec décision < 2 semaines
-  - [x] `ProductList.jsx` — Ajouter un filtre par état (Tous / À gérer / Soldé / Donné / Jeté)
-  - [x] `ProductList.jsx` — Logger la suppression d'un produit (`addLog`, catégorie `'inventory'`)
-
-- [x] **Historique global amélioré** :
-  - [x] `useLogs.js` — Ajouter paramètre `category` à `addLog` ; augmenter la limite de 10 à 500
-  - [x] `ActivityLog.jsx` — Refactoriser en composant réutilisable avec prop `defaultCategory`
-  - [x] `ActivityLog.jsx` — Filtre catégorie (Tous / Inventaire / Nettoyage / Tables / Système)
-  - [x] `ActivityLog.jsx` — Filtres timespan presets : 10 min / 1h / 24h / 7j / 30j / Personnalisé
-  - [x] `ActivityLog.jsx` — Mode Personnalisé : deux `<input datetime-local>` (début / fin)
-  - [x] `CleaningChecklist.jsx` — Ajouter `category: 'cleaning'` à tous les `addLog` existants
-  - [x] `CleaningChecklist.jsx` — Ajouter `<ActivityLog defaultCategory="cleaning" />` en bas de page
-  - [x] `ProductDecisions.jsx` — Logger les décisions (`addLog`, catégorie `'inventory'`)
-  - [x] `DishForm.jsx` — Logger la création d'un plat (`addLog`, catégorie `'inventory'`)
-  - [x] `DishList.jsx` — Logger la suppression d'un plat (`addLog`, catégorie `'inventory'`)
-  - [x] `InventoryManagementDashboard.jsx` — Ajouter onglet "Historique" avec `<ActivityLog defaultCategory="inventory" />`
-
-- [ ] **Historique — Logs manquants** :
-  - [ ] `TableGrid.jsx` — Aucun événement n'est loggé pour les tables. Identifier les actions à tracer (occupation, libération, demandes) et ajouter les `addLog` avec catégorie `'tables'`.
-
-- [ ] **Cohérence de nomenclature** *(décision + implémentation)* :
-  - [ ] Décider d'un nom pour la page `/products` (actuellement `InventoryManagementDashboard`) : regroupe les **ingrédients** (appelés "produits") et les **plats**. Proposition : "Gestion des Stocks" ou "Cuisine".
-  - [ ] Décider d'un nom pour la carte `Inventory` du dashboard : représente le **stock bar/salle** (hors produits cuisine). Proposition : "Stock Salle" ou "Inventaire Bar".
-  - [ ] Une fois les noms choisis : renommer les composants, routes, titres, et labels du filtre catégorie dans `ActivityLog.jsx` en conséquence.
-  - [ ] Renommer la catégorie de log `'system'` en `'stock'` (ou le nom retenu pour la carte Inventory) dans `useLogs.js` et `ActivityLog.jsx`.

+> ⚠️ **Sécurité** : Token API Zenchef et Restaurant ID à stocker dans les variables d'environnement (ne pas committer en clair).

+## Fixes
+
+- [ ] **Multi-device login** : Se connecter avec un même compte depuis un appareil différent crée un second utilisateur en base sans droits admin. Il faut accorder le même rôle au nouveau UID.
+
+- [x] **Tâche expirée** : Quand une tâche expire automatiquement, la barre passe au vert, affiche "Il reste 30 secondes" et la date du prochain nettoyage change toutes les secondes.

-                    <span 
-                        class="${styleClass}"

-                    >
-                        ${icon}
-                    </span>`;

-

-        button.style.backgroundColor = '';
-    });
+export const TABLE_IDS = ['1', '2', '3', '4']; // Ajoutez la liste complète de vos IDs ici
+
+export const INDICATOR_ICONS = {
+    bill: { icon: '🧾', title: "Demande d'Addition" },
+    order: { icon: '📝', title: "Nouvelle Commande" },
+    tpe: { icon: '💳', title: "Demande TPE" },
+};
+
+export const INDICATOR_ORDER = ['order','bill',  'tpe']; 
+
+
+export function updateTableUI(tableRequests) {
+
+    const BASE_CLASSES = "table-card table-button w-full py-3 rounded-lg text-sm font-bold uppercase transition relative overflow-hidden";
+    
+    TABLE_IDS.forEach(tableId => {
+        const button = document.getElementById(`table-${tableId}`);
+        const requests = tableRequests[tableId] || {}; 
+        if (!button) return;
+
+        const hasCriticalRequest = requests.bill === true || requests.tpe === true;
+        const hasOrderRequest = requests.order === true;
+        const tableNumberDisplay = `TABLE ${tableId} (x2)`; 
+        
+        if (hasCriticalRequest) {
+            button.className = `${BASE_CLASSES} bg-mictlan-red text-white border-2 border-white`;
+        } else if (hasOrderRequest) {
+             button.className = `${BASE_CLASSES} bg-orange-500 text-white hover:bg-orange-600`;
+        } else {
+            button.className = `${BASE_CLASSES} bg-green-700 text-white hover:bg-green-600`;
+        }
+        
+        let nameHTML = `<div class="table-name-area">${tableNumberDisplay}</div>`;
+        let indicatorsHTML = '<div class="table-indicators-row">';
+        
+        INDICATOR_ORDER.forEach(type => { 
+            const isActive = requests[type] === true;
+            const { icon, title } = INDICATOR_ICONS[type] || {}; 
+            const styleClass = `indicator ${type}-indicator ${isActive ? 'active' : 'inactive'}`;
+
+            if (icon) {
+                indicatorsHTML += `
+                    <span 
+                        class="${styleClass}" 
+                        title="${title}"
+                        onclick="window.handleIndicatorToggle('${tableId}', '${type}', event)"
+                    >
+                        ${icon}
+                    </span>`;
+            }
+        });
+
+        indicatorsHTML += '</div>';
+        
+        button.setAttribute('onclick', `window.openSingleTableView('${tableId}', event)`);
+        button.innerHTML = nameHTML + indicatorsHTML;
+        button.style.backgroundColor = '';
+    });
 }

+// src/versionInfo.js
+
+import packageJson from '../package.json';
+
+const getAppVersionInfo = () => {
+  const version = packageJson.version;
+  const buildTimestamp = import.meta.env.VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP || 'N/A';
+  const mode = import.meta.env.MODE;
+
+  return {
+    version: version,
+    buildTimestamp: buildTimestamp,
+    mode: mode,
+    fullVersion: `Version ${version} (${buildTimestamp} - ${mode})`
+  };
+};
+
+// Expose globally for easy access (e.g., via browser console)
+window.getAppVersionInfo = getAppVersionInfo;
+
 export default getAppVersionInfo;
